revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 8 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 8-ngie̍t]][[ja:8月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 8월]][[th:เดือน 8]][[za:Ndwenbet]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 8 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:八月]][[zh:农历八月]]
{{Lunisolar August|qq=}} '''Lunisolar August''' (旧暦八月) is the eighth {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}). The month that includes the {{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|qid=Q1200436|lt=Autumnal Equinox|1=Qiufen}} becomes August. In the {{ill|11 Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11 Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11 Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11 Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|August|en|August|qid=Q122|lt=August|1=August}} to early {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}}. Another name for August is {{nihongo|'''Hazuki'''|はづき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|August|ja|8月|qq=draft}}. In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}}, varies by year (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), there are years in which August ends on the 29th, with no August 30th. ==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|August (Lunisolar)|ja|8月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar August|lt_ja=旧暦8月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Hazuki''' (はづき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for August of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=The new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}. There are various theories about the origin of the name Hazuki. The most famous holds that it comes from the term “Ha-ochi-zuki” (葉落ち月), “leaf-fall month,” when the {{ill|Leaf|en|Leaf|qid=Q33971|lt=leaves|1=Leaf}} of the {{ill|trees|ja|Wood (木)|1=Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|lt=Wood (木)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/trees|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/trees|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/trees|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} turn color ({{ill|Autumn leaf color|en|Autumn leaf color|qid=Q1156320|lt=autumn leaves|1=Autumn leaf color}}) and fall. Other theories propose that it derives from “Hohari-zuki” (穂張月; ほはりづき), “ear-expansion month,” when rice ear stalks swell; or from “Hatsukizuki” (初来月; はつきづき), “first-arrival month,” referring to the first arrival of {{ill|Goose|en|Goose|qid=Q16529344|lt=wild geese|1=Goose}}; or from “Haezuki” (南風月; はえづき), “south-wind month,” when many {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|qid=Q140588|lt=typhoons|1=Typhoon}} come from the south. Another alternate name is “Tsukimizuki” (月見月; つきみづき).
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, August is called “Kunnesusichup” (クンネスシチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: kunne susi cup), meaning “month of bathing in water at night”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
== Hazuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Hazuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! 11 Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || {{ill|August 25|en|August 25|qid=Q2819|lt=August 25|1=August 25}} || {{ill|September 23|en|September 23|qid=Q2888|lt=September 23|1=September 23}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || {{ill|September 13|en|September 13|qid=Q2848|lt=September 13|1=September 13}} || {{ill|October 12|en|October 12|qid=Q2920|lt=October 12|1=October 12}} || 30 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|September 1|en|September 1|qid=Q2859|lt=September 1|1=September 1}} || {{ill|September 30|en|September 30|qid=Q2881|lt=September 30|1=September 30}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|qid=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}} || {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|qid=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|qid=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} || {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|qid=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}} || 29 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|August 30|en|August 30|qid=Q2818|lt=August 30|1=August 30}} || {{ill|September 28|en|September 28|qid=Q2883|lt=September 28|1=September 28}} || 30 days |- | 2020 || {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|qid=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}} || {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|qid=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}} || 30 days |- | 2021 || {{ill|September 7|en|September 7|qid=Q2852|lt=September 7|1=September 7}} || {{ill|October 5|en|October 5|qid=Q2930|lt=October 5|1=October 5}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|August 27|en|August 27|qid=Q2822|lt=August 27|1=August 27}} || {{ill|September 25|en|September 25|qid=Q2886|lt=September 25|1=September 25}} || 30 days |- | 2023 || {{ill|September 15|en|September 15|qid=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} || {{ill|October 14|en|October 14|qid=Q2922|lt=October 14|1=October 14}} || 30 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|September 3|en|September 3|qid=Q2860|lt=September 3|1=September 3}} || {{ill|October 2|en|October 2|qid=Q2932|lt=October 2|1=October 2}} || 30 days |- | 2025 || {{ill|September 22|en|September 22|qid=Q2889|lt=September 22|1=September 22}} || {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|qid=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}} || 29 days |}
